findings.title: CI test command is not green in the reviewed worktree
findings.description: The CI workflow runs bun test, and that command currently exits nonzero from the reviewed worktree. Bun discovers the artifact POC test file under artifacts/smithering/poc/safety-hook-approval-roundtrip/ and the run fails with three local server startup errors. This violates the ticket requirement that bun test run green in CI and makes the archived tests.log unreliable for this tree.

findings.title: Exact required smoke command fails
findings.description: The ticket verification names bun test test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts. Running that exact command fails because Bun treats the argument as a filter for this nonstandard filename; only the ./-prefixed variant runs. The gate evidence acknowledges the alternate command, but the required command is not satisfied.

findings.title: Evidence bundle is stale and incomplete
findings.description: verify.json still reports the old smoke filename, old pass counts, and a full-suite green result that does not match the current worktree. In addition, the tsc and secret-scan green evidence logs are empty, and gates.json marks the full-suite pre-merge gate without an RBG pair. The mandate requires genuine red-before-green pairs in evidence for blocking gates.

findings.title: Smoke assertion barely proves a structured LogEvent
findings.description: The smoke test parses the single emitted line and checks only correlationId. It does not assert the LogEvent shape fields such as event, sessionId, level, or meta, so a non-LogEvent JSON line with a correlationId would satisfy the test even though the ticket asks for one structured trace line.
